records.ety: [Perhaps fr. F. quille ninepin (see Kayless); but cf. also G. kiel a quill. MHG. kil, and Ir. cuille a quill.]

defs: One of the large feathers of a bird's wing, or one of the rectrices of the tail; also, the stock of such a feather.
A pen for writing made by sharpening and splitting the point or nib of the stock of a feather; as, history is the proper subject of his quill.
A spine of the hedgehog or porcupine.
The pen of a squid. See Pen.
The plectrum with which musicians strike the strings of certain instruments.
The tube of a musical instrument.
quotes: He touched the tender stops of various quills.

defs: Something having the form of a quill
The fold or plain of a ruff.
A spindle, or spool, as of reed or wood, upon which the thread for the woof is wound in a shuttle.
A roll of dried bark; as, a quill of cinnamon or of cinchona.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Quilled (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Quilling.]

defs: To plaint in small cylindrical ridges, called quillings; as, to quill a ruffle.
quotes: His cravat seemed quilled into a ruff.

defs: To wind on a quill, as thread or yarn.
